findings.title: Critical Accessibility Gaps for Visually Impaired Users
findings.description: Visually impaired users are unable to navigate the course catalog or complete the enrollment flow due to the lack of ARIA roles and keyboard support. This significantly hinders their ability to access essential features of the platform.
assumptions: Accessibility is a requirement for all users.

evidence.excerpt: Implement ARIA roles and keyboard support for the course catalog and enrollment flow.

recommendations: Implement ARIA roles and keyboard support for the course catalog and enrollment flow.

metadata.mitigation: Implement ARIA roles and keyboard support.

findings.title: Video Player Accessibility Enhancements Needed
findings.description: The absence of closed captions, transcripts, and keyboard controls in the video player blocks access for deaf, hard-of-hearing, and motor-impaired users, violating multiple WCAG standards.
assumptions: Accessibility features are essential for all users.

evidence.excerpt: Add closed captions, transcripts, and keyboard controls to the video player.

recommendations: Add closed captions, transcripts, and keyboard controls to the video player.

metadata.mitigation: Enhance the video player for accessibility.

findings.title: Color Contrast Failures Across UI Components
findings.description: 60% of UI components fail to meet WCAG AA contrast ratios, which can lead to accessibility issues for users with visual impairments.
assumptions: Color contrast is critical for accessibility.

evidence.excerpt: Revise UI components to meet WCAG AA contrast ratios.

recommendations: Revise UI components to meet WCAG AA contrast ratios.

metadata.mitigation: Revise UI components for better contrast.

findings.title: Inaccessible Quiz and Assessment Forms
findings.description: Quiz forms are not accessible to assistive technologies, making it impossible for users with disabilities to participate in assessments.

evidence.excerpt: Make quiz forms accessible to assistive technologies.

recommendations: Make quiz forms accessible to assistive technologies.

findings.title: Accessibility Compliance Risk
findings.description: The current system has significant accessibility gaps that violate WCAG 2.1 AA standards, which poses a high risk for compliance and user experience. Immediate remediation is required to avoid legal repercussions and to serve users with disabilities effectively.
assumptions: Compliance with accessibility standards is mandatory.

evidence.excerpt: Significant accessibility gaps that violate WCAG 2.1 AA standards.

recommendations: Conduct a full accessibility audit and implement necessary changes.

summary: Identified 5 risks (5 accessibility). 5 flagged as blockers. Risk gate: FAILED (6 blockers, 0 warnings).
